name: El Molar, El Vellon, Torrelaguna, El Berrueco - circular route
name_original: El Molar, El Vellón, Torrelaguna, El Berrueco – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pedrezuela traverse diverse landscapes within the picturesque Lozoya Somosierra region of Madrid. The area features undulating roads, rolling hills, and challenging mountain passes, particularly within the Sierra Norte. Cyclists encounter varied vegetation and well-paved surfaces, offering a mix of flat sections and ascents. This region provides a suitable environment for road bikes, connecting charming towns through scenic mountains and valleys. Best road cycling routes around Pedrezuela The most popular road cycling route is El Molar, El Vellon, Torrelaguna, El Berrueco - circular route, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route offers scenic mountain and valley roads with varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santillana Reservoir – Alto del Cerro de San Pedro loop from El Molar, a moderate 47.4 miles (76.3 km) path. This route provides views of the Pedrezuela Reservoir and surrounding hills. Local road cyclists also love the Canencia Pass – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from El Molar, a 50.4 miles (81.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra Norte, often completed in about 4 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Pedrezuela is defined by undulating roads, rolling hills, and mountain passes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Pedrezuela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 2,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pedrezuela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pedrezuela?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes around Pedrezuela, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 2,600 times.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Pedrezuela?
A: Pedrezuela offers a diverse range of road cycling routes. You'll find 1 easy route, 34 moderate routes, and 25 difficult routes, catering to cyclists from beginners to experienced riders looking for a challenge.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along road cycling routes in Pedrezuela?
A: Many road cycling routes in Pedrezuela pass by beautiful natural attractions. You can incorporate stops at the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/484448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the refreshing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2702893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Merodio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on road cycling routes around Pedrezuela?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning views, including those of the Sierra de Guadarrama. You can also find beautiful vistas of the reservoir from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pedrezuela Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Pedrezuela?
A: The road cycling routes around Pedrezuela are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, well-paved roads, and the blend of flat sections, rolling hills, and challenging ascents within the Sierra Norte.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Pedrezuela?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977042179/el-molar-el-vellon-torrelaguna-el-berrueco-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Molar, El Vellon, Torrelaguna, El Berrueco - circular route&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic mountain and valley roads. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from El Molar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the road conditions like for cycling in Pedrezuela?
A: The region surrounding Pedrezuela, particularly within the Sierra Norte, is known for its well-paved roads and adapted infrastructure. While specific road quality within Pedrezuela itself isn't extensively detailed, routes in the area are generally described as having mostly well-paved surfaces, making them suitable for road bikes.

Q: Are there options for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Pedrezuela offers routes suitable for various skill levels. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. These easier options provide a good starting point for beginners to enjoy the scenic surroundings.

Q: Can I find routes with cafes or restaurants along the way?
A: While specific cafes or restaurants are not detailed for each route, Pedrezuela and the surrounding towns are part of a cycling-friendly region. Many routes connect charming towns, making it likely to find places to stop for refreshments or a meal. The broader Madrid region also supports cycling tourism with various services.

Q: Is public transport available to reach road cycling routes in Pedrezuela?
A: The region's commitment to cycling tourism suggests accessibility, but specific public transport options directly to trailheads with bike carriage facilities are not detailed. It's advisable to check local bus or train services for routes connecting to Pedrezuela and their policies regarding bicycle transport.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes around Pedrezuela?
A: Yes, you can find several longer routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31806217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir – Alto del Cerro de San Pedro loop from El Molar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 76.3 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31804816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vellón – El Molar loop from El Molar&lt;/a&gt; covers 73.8 km, offering extensive rides through the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pedrezuela?
A: The region's diverse landscapes and generally favorable road conditions make it suitable for cycling across many seasons. However, for optimal comfort, spring and autumn typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant weather, ideal for exploring the Sierra Norte's mountains and valleys.
tips: One of the accesses to the Madrid mountains, has an altitude of 850 meters. El Molar is known for its caves, which number more than two hundred. Some are of medieval and Muslim origin. In many of them, the wines produced in the town mature. Others have been used for the installation of restaurants, with their typical dishes such as blood sausage soup, sheep stew or lamb with salt and garlic.

It is a beautiful church, in the Gothic style, built in the 14th century and one of the best exponents of Elizabethan or late Gothic in the peninsula, and especially in the Community of Madrid. The altar is something incredible! Only open on Saturdays and Sundays.

A nice summer place. The origin of the town may be Arab since it has an archaeological remains (a watchtower) of Arab origin. Its numerous springs and fountains stand out, famous for their fine waters.
